mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low interest rates reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings growth, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 60% of high-GDP-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 50% of instances when inflation crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust job creation and labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative returns with t-stat below 1.5 in late-cycle periods.
